methods.description: This method involves the analysis of pollen samples from sediment cores to understand historical vegetation and environmental conditions. It includes sample collection, preparation, and microscopic analysis to identify and count pollen types.

steps.description: For the pollen analysis subsamples of the first 360 cm sediment core (WA-1) and the lowest part (360 cm-425 cm) of the second 425 cm sediment core (WA-2) were taken.
steps.searchable_summary: collect subsamples from sediment cores WA-1 and WA-2

steps.description: In a first step subsamples from every 5-6 cm were selected and analysed.
steps.searchable_summary: select and analyze subsamples every 5-6 cm

steps.description: In a second step, after reviewing preliminary pollen data, areas of special importance were selected, and further subsamples were taken at these selected depths.
steps.searchable_summary: select additional subsamples from areas of interest

steps.description: The samples were treated following the standard acetolysis method.
steps.searchable_summary: apply standard acetolysis method to samples

steps.description: After treatment with 10% HCl and 10% KOH, samples were sieved with 160 µm mesh size.
steps.searchable_summary: treat samples with HCl and KOH, then sieve

steps.description: Treatment with concentrated cold HF for 48 h prior to acetolysis was used for samples with high mineral content.
steps.searchable_summary: treat high mineral content samples with cold HF

steps.description: Pollen and spores were identified under the light microscope at 160×-1000× magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen and spores using light microscopy

steps.description: A minimum of 350 pollen was counted per slide and a total of 94 pollen types was identified.
steps.searchable_summary: count and identify pollen types per slide

steps.description: The sum for calculation of pollen percentages includes trees, shrubs, and herbs, whereas spores and Cyperaceae pollen were excluded.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pollen percentages excluding spores and Cyperaceae

steps.description: Linear regression analysis between Plantago lanceolata and other potential indicator species was performed.
steps.searchable_summary: perform linear regression analysis on indicator species

methods.description: This method involves the use of NMDS and cluster analysis to understand the chronological distribution of pollen samples and their correlation with cultural epochs.

steps.description: The NMDS graph partly reflects the chronology of cultural epochs and shows a correlation of the pollen spectrum with time.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze NMDS graph for pollen spectrum correlation with time

steps.description: The Broken Stick Model based on the CONISS cluster analysis identified two main pollen zones.
steps.searchable_summary: use CONISS cluster analysis to identify pollen zones
